skill.name: planning-with-files
skill.description: Use persistent markdown files to plan complex work, track progress, store notes, and produce deliverables across long AI sessions.
skill.summary: Plan long tasks with persistent markdown files for goals, notes, progress, and final deliverables.
skill.author: OthmanAdi

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak crypto, browser storage, URL, and dangerous-combination patterns. Review found these are markdown examples, inline filenames, and one documentation link, not executable code or data exfiltration. The only confirmed concern is intentional filesystem use to create and update planning files, so publication is safe with a low-risk label.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Planning Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill intentionally directs agents to create, read, write, and edit markdown planning files in the working directory. This is expected behavior, but users should understand it can add or modify files during complex tasks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The core instructions explicitly require creating and updating markdown files. The behavior is clear and legitimate, but it is still filesystem modification.

low_findings.title: Markdown Syntax Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings point to markdown code fences, inline filenames, and pseudocode examples. No executable Ruby, shell backtick expression, or command injection path is present in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation syntax or examples, not runnable source code. There is no user-controlled command construction or execution API.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Not Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: The weak crypto alerts occur in prose and markdown planning content. Review found no MD5, SHA1, hashing implementation, password storage, or cryptographic operation at the flagged locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are descriptive markdown, not cryptographic code. No weak algorithm name is used as an algorithm call.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Mention Is a Hypothetical Example
low_findings.description: The localStorage reference appears inside an example plan for adding a dark mode toggle. It does not instruct the skill to read browser storage or extract user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a sample decision in a feature-development plan. There is no active browser script or data access logic.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Is Not Network Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a source citation for context-engineering documentation. The skill does not make a network request or send data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is presented as a reference link only. No fetch, curl, webhook, or automated request behavior appears nearby.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in conceptual planning guidance, not in commands that enumerate system details. No host, process, network, or credential discovery instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are section headings or table text about workflow quality. They do not perform or request system discovery.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined false positives for code execution, network access, and credential access. Manual review found no executable code, no data exfiltration, no credential collection, and no prompt-injection attempt.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each component signal was reviewed in context and found to be documentation or example text. The combined risk is therefore not supported by semantic evidence.

content.user_title: Plan Complex Work with Persistent Files
content.value_statement: Long AI tasks can lose goals, decisions, and research details across many tool calls. This skill uses markdown planning files to preserve context, track progress, and organize deliverables.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of task_plan.md before complex tasks.
Defines a three-file pattern for plans, notes, and deliverables.
Prompts agents to update progress after each phase.
Encourages storing research findings in notes.md instead of chat context.
Provides examples for research, bug fixes, feature work, and error recovery.
Documents when to use or skip the planning workflow.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not provide executable automation.
It can create extra markdown files in the current workspace.
It is unnecessary for simple questions or very small edits.
It does not validate the accuracy of research sources or final deliverables.

use_cases.title: Manage Long Coding Tasks
use_cases.description: Track goals, root-cause notes, implementation phases, and verification steps while fixing bugs or building features.

use_cases.title: Organize Research Work
use_cases.description: Collect source notes, synthesize findings, and preserve decisions before writing a final summary.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Step Deliverables
use_cases.description: Break complex requests into phases and keep status visible until the final output is complete.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a complex request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-with-files workflow for this task. Create a task_plan.md with the goal, phases, key questions, and current status before you begin.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Research and Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use task_plan.md and notes.md for this research task. Save source findings in notes.md, update the plan after each phase, and deliver a concise final summary.

prompt_templates.title: Track a Coding Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the three-file pattern for this coding task. Keep root-cause findings, decisions, errors, and verification steps in persistent markdown files as you work.

prompt_templates.title: Recover and Continue Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced continuation after many tool calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the existing task_plan.md and notes.md, identify the current phase, update any stale status, then continue the task without losing prior decisions.

output_examples.input: Research a topic and produce a brief executive summary.
output_examples.output: A task plan lists the research phases, a notes file captures source findings, and the final summary references the saved findings.

output_examples.input: Fix a login bug in an existing codebase.
output_examples.output: The plan records the investigation path, root cause, chosen fix, errors encountered, and verification status before final delivery.

output_examples.input: Continue a complex task after a long session.
output_examples.output: The agent rereads the plan, restores the current goal and phase, updates progress, and continues from the latest documented decision.

best_practices: Use this workflow for tasks with three or more meaningful phases.
Keep plans short enough to reread often during the task.
Record errors, decisions, and source notes as soon as they happen.
anti_patterns: Do not use this workflow for simple one-step answers.
Do not store sensitive secrets in planning or notes files.
Do not let the plan replace final testing or source verification.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to use markdown files for plans, notes, progress tracking, and final deliverables.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain workflow instructions and examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Will it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. Its intended workflow creates and updates markdown files in the working directory for complex tasks.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for research, coding, writing, or operational tasks that require several phases and durable context.

faq.question: When should I skip it?
faq.answer: Skip it for quick questions, simple edits, or tasks where extra planning files would add noise.

faq.question: Does it protect sensitive data?
faq.answer: No. Users should avoid placing secrets, passwords, tokens, or private data in generated planning files.
